Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Discover which deals Catalina and Mina were working on by searching emails for their correspondence. Search emails using search_emails (query: "Catalina" or "Catalina Dubois") across both INBOX and SENT folders to find Catalina's deal-related outbound threads. Expected findings: among Catalina's emails, the agent discovers outbound correspondence with Harbour Pharmaceutical (c.fontaine@harbourpharma.com) about employee relocation for pharmaceutical teams. Similarly, search emails using search_emails (query: "Mina" or "Mina Hashimoto") to find Mina's deal-related threads. Expected findings: among Mina's emails, the agent discovers outbound correspondence with Driftwood Analytics (r.huang@driftwoodanalytics.com) about remote relocation for data teams. These discoveries establish the company names needed for deeper investigation. Alternatively, the agent could discover these by searching Airtable client accounts using airtable_search_records (base_id, table_name: "tblClientAccts01", field_name: "Account Manager", value: "Catalina Dubois" or "Mina Hashimoto") or by browsing CRM deals.
OE 2: Search emails for Harbour Pharmaceutical correspondence using search_emails (query: "Harbour Pharmaceutical" or "Harbour" or "harbourpharma") in both INBOX and SENT folders. Expected findings: Catalina Dubois's initial cold outreach email to c.fontaine@harbourpharma.com about employee relocation for pharmaceutical teams, Catalina's follow-up email circling back, a reply from d.kowalski@harbourpharma.com (signed by Claire Fontaine, Global Mobility Manager) requesting help with an urgent senior computational biologist relocation from Cambridge MA to Philadelphia by May 1st (furnished apartment in University City or Rittenhouse, flight/Amtrak coordination, home-office setup), and Catalina's detailed proposal email offering the Professional tier at $4,800. No further reply from Harbour after Catalina's proposal — the conversation went silent.
OE 3: Search emails for Driftwood Analytics correspondence using search_emails (query: "Driftwood Analytics" or "Driftwood" or "driftwoodanalytics") in both INBOX and SENT folders. Expected findings: Mina Hashimoto's initial cold outreach email to r.huang@driftwoodanalytics.com, a reply from k.watanabe@driftwoodanalytics.com (Kenji Watanabe, Director of People) expressing interest and asking about pricing, tiered service levels, lead time, and outbound move coverage, and Mina's detailed response to r.huang@driftwoodanalytics.com addressing all four questions and proposing a 30-minute discovery call ("Does next Tuesday or Wednesday afternoon work?"). No reply from Driftwood after Mina's proposal , the discovery call was never confirmed.
OE 4: Search Slack for any Harbour Pharmaceutical or Driftwood Analytics mentions using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Harbour" or "Harbour Pharmaceutical"). Then search again with search_query: "Driftwood" or "Driftwood Analytics". Expected finding: no Slack messages in any channel mention either Harbour Pharmaceutical or Driftwood Analytics, confirming there was no internal discussion about these deals.
OE 5: Search CRM for the Harbour Pharmaceutical company record using crm_search_companies (name: "Harbour Pharmaceutical" or "Harbour"). Expected findings: company_harbour, Harbour Pharmaceutical, type "Prospect", Philadelphia PA, Pharmaceuticals industry, 1,400 employees, $210M annual revenue. Then search for associated deals using crm_search_deals (company_id: "company_harbour" or dealname: "Harbour"). Expected finding: deal_harbour_001,"Harbour Pharmaceutical, Boston to Philadelphia Senior Researcher Relocation", amount $4,800, dealstage "qualifiedtobuy", close date 2026-04-15 (mid-April, already past), pipeline "Sales", description noting urgent single-move engagement, professional tier proposal sent, awaiting confirmation call from Claire Fontaine. The deal is stale,close date has passed with no progress.
OE 6: Search CRM for the Driftwood Analytics company record using crm_search_companies (name: "Driftwood Analytics" or "Driftwood"). Expected findings: company_driftwood, Driftwood Analytics, type "Prospect", Seattle WA, Data Analytics industry, 175 employees. Then search for associated deals using crm_search_deals (company_id: "company_driftwood" or dealname: "Driftwood"). Expected finding: deal_driftwood_001, "Driftwood Analytics,Relocation Program Discovery", amount $15,000, dealstage "appointmentscheduled", close date 2026-05-31, pipeline "Sales", description noting warm inbound from Rachel Huang, 5–8 moves/year estimated, discovery call proposed for next week. The deal is stale, deal was created 2026-03-12, stage is still "appointmentscheduled" with no advancement, and the discovery call was never scheduled.
OE 7: Search calendar for any existing discovery call or meeting related to Driftwood Analytics using calendar_search_events (query: "Driftwood" or "discovery" or "Rachel Huang" or "Kenji Watanabe"). Expected finding: no calendar events exist for Driftwood, confirming Mina never scheduled the discovery call she proposed in her email.
OE 8: Look up Catalina Dubois's email address using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Catalina Dubois" or "Catalina"). Expected finding: catalina.dubois@moveops.com. Also look up Mina Hashimoto's email address using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Mina Hashimoto" or "Mina"). Expected finding: mina.hashimoto@moveops.com. These are needed for the send_email and calendar_add_calendar_event actions.
OE 9: Update CRM deal notes for Harbour Pharmaceutical by creating an engagement note using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", body: summary of investigation findings, close date of April 15 has passed, no reply from Claire Fontaine after Catalina's proposal, deal is stale, May 1 relocation deadline approaching, company_ids: ["company_harbour"]). This documents the real status of the deal in CRM.
OE 10: Update CRM deal notes for Driftwood Analytics by creating an engagement note using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", body: summary of investigation findings, Kenji Watanabe expressed interest, Mina proposed a discovery call but received no reply, call never scheduled, deal sitting at "appointmentscheduled" since March, prospect may have gone cold, company_ids: ["company_driftwood"]). This documents the real status of the deal in CRM.
OE 11: Send email to Catalina Dubois using send_email (sender: david.chen@moveops.com, recipients: ["catalina.dubois@moveops.com"], subject: about the Harbour Pharmaceutical deal being overdue, content: letting Catalina know that David noticed the Harbour deal looks overdue, the close date of mid-April has passed, asking what happened with the researcher relocation to Philadelphia, noting that if the May 1 deadline is still possible they are running out of time and need to act immediately).
OE 12: Look up the customer-engagement Slack channel ID using channels_list (channel_types: "public_channel"). Expected finding: channel C002 is "customer-engagement". This is needed for the conversations_add_message action.
OE 13: Post a pipeline status update in the #customer-engagement Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C002", payload: pipeline status update covering the findings on Harbour Pharmaceutical and Driftwood Analytics, Harbour deal is past its close date with no client response, Driftwood discovery call never scheduled and prospect may be cold, David is reviewing pipeline and paying attention to new business again).
OE 14: Create a calendar event for a pipeline review meeting on Monday morning using calendar_add_calendar_event (title: pipeline review or similar, start_datetime: Monday April 27 2026 at a reasonable morning time in Pacific timezone, attendees: ["mina.hashimoto@moveops.com", "catalina.dubois@moveops.com"], description: full pipeline review meeting with Mina and Catalina to go through the pipeline). The agent must determine that the next Monday is April 27, 2026 and select an appropriate morning time slot.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The Harbour note body explicitly states: 'The close date of April 15 has passed' and 'No follow-up call was scheduled, no further emails were sent, and no engagement was logged in CRM.' Both required facts are present.

✗Run #4No CRM engagement note was created for Driftwood Analytics. The agent created a note for Foxglove Biotech (company_foxglove) which is a different company. There is no note for company_driftwood mentioning a stalled discovery call or the appointmentscheduled stage.

✓Run #6The Driftwood note body states: 'Discovery call proposed by Mina Hashimoto in January was never scheduled — no response from Kenji Watanabe or Rachel Huang since Mina's detailed follow-up. Over 3 months of inactivity.' and explicitly mentions keeping it at 'appointmentscheduled'.
5 An email was sent (via send_email) from [david.chen@moveops.com](mailto:david.chen@moveops.com) to [catalina.dubois@moveops.com](mailto:catalina.dubois@moveops.com) regarding the Harbour Pharmaceutical deal and the tool returned success.
